description: JPMorgan Chase is mandating AI adoption for its 65,000 software engineers, demanding measurable improvements in code quality and productivity.  Internal tracking monitors tool usage, with lagging employees risking underperformance reviews. This push aligns with a broader industry trend, as tech giants integrate AI into performance evaluations and promotion criteria.

Microsoft AI CEO reveals what will really decide AI's winners and losers by 2028
description: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman asserts that the AI industry's future hinges on who can afford to run models at scale, not just who builds the smartest ones.  He argues that inference compute scarcity will define winners for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant edge through a data-driven improvement flywheel.

title: Google founder to employees at town hall: This year a big role will be played by AI agents
description: Google employees are buzzing about Agent Smith, an internal AI tool automating tasks and accessible via mobile. Its popularity led to restricted access, highlighting Google's focus on AI agents for productivity in 2026, as emphasized by cofounder Sergey Brin. AI adoption is becoming mandatory across the company, with usage even impacting performance reviews.

title: TJ Watt’s $123M deal criticized as Aaron Rodgers eyes new contract
description: The Pittsburgh Steelers face a significant financial challenge in the 2026 offseason. With T.J. Watt's hefty contract and Aaron Rodgers seeking a substantial raise, the Steelers' cap space is severely strained. Watt's production has declined, raising questions about his value, while Rodgers' strong performance has inflated his market, forcing a tough decision on which star to prioritise. Now, they must choose. Stick with a high-priced defence led by Watt or adjust the books to bring Rodgers back. Either way, the margin for error is gone.
